title: mangifera timorensis
intro: Mangifera timorensis, also known as the Timor mango or Timor mangrove, is a species of plant in the family Anacardiaceae. It is a flowering plant native to Timor Island in Indonesia and has been used for its medicinal properties for centuries. The bark, leaves, and roots of the Mangifera timorensis plant have been traditionally used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant can be prepared as a decoction, tea, or powder, and its health benefits include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system.
